# Smart Campus API cURL Commands
# You can import these directly into Postman by going to File -> Import -> Raw text and pasting the cURL commands.
# NOTE: Replace the UUIDs (roomId, sensorId) with actual IDs generated by your POST requests.
# The default base URL is assumed to be http://localhost:8080/api (Update the port and base path if your server runs on a different one).

### ROOMS ###

# 1. Get All Rooms
curl --location --request GET 'http://localhost:8080/api/rooms'

# 2. Create a Room
curl --location --request POST 'http://localhost:8080/api/rooms' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{
    "name": "Server Room A",
    "capacity": 50
}'

# 3. Get a Specific Room
# Replace {roomId} with an actual ID from the Get All Rooms response
curl --location --request GET 'http://localhost:8080/api/rooms/{roomId}'

# 4. Delete a Room
# Replace {roomId} with an actual ID
curl --location --request DELETE 'http://localhost:8080/api/rooms/{roomId}'


### SENSORS ###

# 5. Get All Sensors (Optional: filter by type, e.g., ?type=TEMPERATURE)
curl --location --request GET 'http://localhost:8080/api/sensors'

# 6. Create a Sensor
# You must provide a valid roomId from an existing room
curl --location --request POST 'http://localhost:8080/api/sensors' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{
    "roomId": "{roomId}",
    "type": "TEMPERATURE",
    "status": "ACTIVE",
    "currentValue": 0.0
}'

# 6b. Delete a Sensor
# Replace {sensorId} with an actual sensor ID
curl --location --request DELETE 'http://localhost:8080/api/sensors/{sensorId}'


### SENSOR READINGS ###

# 7. Get Reading History for a Sensor
# Replace {sensorId} with an actual sensor ID
curl --location --request GET 'http://localhost:8080/api/sensors/{sensorId}/readings'

# 8. Append a new Reading to a Sensor
# Replace {sensorId} with an actual sensor ID
curl --location --request POST 'http://localhost:8080/api/sensors/{sensorId}/readings' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{
    "value": 24.5
}'

### ADDITIONAL EXAMPLES FROM COLLEAGUE ###

# Create Active Sensor
curl --location --request POST 'http://localhost:8080/api/sensors' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{
    "roomId": "{roomId}",
    "type": "TEMPERATURE",
    "status": "ACTIVE",
    "currentValue": 0.0
}'

# Create Maintenance Sensor
curl --location --request POST 'http://localhost:8080/api/sensors' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{
    "roomId": "{roomId}",
    "type": "CO2",
    "status": "MAINTENANCE",
    "currentValue": 0.0
}'

# Create Sensor With Missing Room (HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity vs Null Room ID)
# Note: As per LinkedResourceNotFoundExceptionMapper (Q7), creating a sensor with a non-existent room ID is a missing reference and should return 422 Unprocessable Entity. However, testing shows it might create a sensor with a null room ID depending on the exact implementation handling.
curl --location --request POST 'http://localhost:8080/api/sensors' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{
    "roomId": "non-existent-room-id",
    "type": "TEMPERATURE",
    "status": "ACTIVE"
}'

# Wrong Content Type Demo (HTTP 415 Unsupported Media Type)
# Note: As per Question 3.1, sending text/plain when the server consumes application/json should return a 415 error, automatically handled by JAX-RS.
curl --location --request POST 'http://localhost:8080/api/rooms' \
--header 'Content-Type: text/plain' \
--data-raw '{"name": "Room A"}'

# List Sensors
curl --location --request GET 'http://localhost:8080/api/sensors'

# Filter Sensors Temperature
curl --location --request GET 'http://localhost:8080/api/sensors?type=TEMPERATURE'

# Filter Sensors CO2
curl --location --request GET 'http://localhost:8080/api/sensors?type=CO2'

# Get Empty Reading History
curl --location --request GET 'http://localhost:8080/api/sensors/{newSensorId}/readings'

# Create Reading For Active Sensor
curl --location --request POST 'http://localhost:8080/api/sensors/{activeSensorId}/readings' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{
    "value": 24.5
}'

# Get Parent Sensor After Reading
curl --location --request GET 'http://localhost:8080/api/sensors/{activeSensorId}'

# Get Reading History After Insert
curl --location --request GET 'http://localhost:8080/api/sensors/{activeSensorId}/readings'

# Get Single Reading
curl --location --request GET 'http://localhost:8080/api/sensors/{activeSensorId}/readings/{readingId}'

# Post Reading To Maintenance Sensor (Testing SensorUnavailableException)
# Note: Should throw a SensorUnavailableException (400 or 409) because the sensor is not in an ACTIVE state.
curl --location --request POST 'http://localhost:8080/api/sensors/{maintenanceSensorId}/readings' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{
    "value": 30.0
}'

# Delete Room That Still Has Sensors (Testing constraints/cascading)
# Note: Might return an error if deleting a room with active sensors violates business logic.
curl --location --request DELETE 'http://localhost:8080/api/rooms/{roomIdWithSensors}'

# Global 500 Demo (Testing GenericExceptionMapper)
# Note: As per Question 6, this tests if the GenericExceptionMapper properly hides the raw Java stack trace when an unexpected error (like invalid JSON syntax) occurs.
curl --location --request POST 'http://localhost:8080/api/rooms' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{"name": "Bad Room", "capacity": '

### MORE EDGE CASES AND ERROR TESTS ###

# DELETE Idempotency Demo (HTTP 204 or 404)
# Note: As per Question 2.2, sending the exact same DELETE request multiple times should yield the same structural system state. The second request might return a 404 Not Found or a 204 No Content.
curl --location --request DELETE 'http://localhost:8080/api/rooms/{deletedRoomId}'

# Get Non-Existent Room (HTTP 404 Not Found)
curl --location --request GET 'http://localhost:8080/api/rooms/invalid-id-123'

# Create Room With Negative Capacity (Testing Validation)
curl --location --request POST 'http://localhost:8080/api/rooms' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{
    "name": "Invalid Room",
    "capacity": -10
}'

# Post Reading to Non-Existent Sensor (HTTP 404)
curl --location --request POST 'http://localhost:8080/api/sensors/non-existent-sensor/readings' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{
    "value": 15.0
}'
